prompt:
Pen-and-watercolor illustration in the style of a contemporary illustrated ski guidebook. Confident black ink contour lines drawn with a fine pen describe every form, then filled with richly saturated watercolor washes — dense, layered, with visible brushstrokes and patches of unpainted white paper showing through within shapes. Visible cross-hatching and stippling in shadows. Crisp edges where ink meets paint. Detailed and storybook-illustrated, not loose or impressionistic. Palette: full-color but rooted in alpine blues, warm earth browns, deep evergreens, fresh snow whites, with a single saturated red accent where the subject calls for it. Center the subject in the frame; allow a thin white deckled paper border around the entire image. Do not exceed the borders of a single panel.

Match the painting style, palette, and texture of the attached reference images exactly.

Subject: Top-down schematic ski-piste base map of Whistler Blackcomb resort, painted in pen and watercolor in the same style as the attached reference panel 5, BUT WITH NO TEXT AND NO ANNOTATIONS WHATSOEVER. Two shaded peaks (Blackcomb on the left, Whistler on the right) rendered in soft graphite-and-watercolor with snow-white tops and shadowed ridgelines. Suggested ski runs as faint, paler corridors winding down the mountainsides. Dark stippled conifer forest covers the lower flanks. A tiny painted village of small red-roofed buildings nestled at the bottom center. Cream paper ground, deckled paper border. Square composition. CRITICAL: absolutely no lettering, no labels, no text of any kind, no pin markers, no numbered circles, no dotted lines, no route arrows — all of those are added afterward as a digital overlay. The painted base must be entirely free of writing or graphic symbols. Just pure painted terrain.
